reasons: Atlas can start a heavy grouped engage, giving Faramis a clear moment to use Nether Realm.
Faramis protects the team during the dangerous follow-up window after Atlas commits.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the grouped engage, while Faramis gives allies survival value during the teamfight collapse window.
worksBestWhen: Atlas engages while Faramis is close enough to cover the follow-up.
Faramis casts Nether Realm before allies are finished by burst.
failureCases: Atlas engages before Faramis is in position.
Enemy waits out Nether Realm before re-engaging.

reasons: Tigreal forces grouped fights where Faramis's teamwide protection has high value.
Faramis helps teammates survive the counter-burst that often follows Tigreal's engage.

proof.summary: Tigreal starts the crowd-control fight, while Faramis protects the team so the follow-up does not collapse immediately.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal and Faramis coordinate engage timing.
Faramis stays close enough to cover teammates following Tigreal.
failureCases: Faramis casts too late after allies are already out of the fight.

reasons: Gloo is a sustain-heavy frontline hero who can stay inside extended fights, making Faramis's protection window valuable.
Faramis can protect Gloo during the risky part of his commit while Gloo applies sticky pressure and absorbs attention.

proof.summary: Gloo wants to keep fighting and disrupting enemies, while Faramis adds survival insurance during Gloo's commit window.
worksBestWhen: Gloo starts pressure while Faramis is close enough to cover the teamfight.
Faramis casts Nether Realm before Gloo or nearby allies are finished by burst.
failureCases: Gloo attaches or commits too far away from the team.
Enemy disengages and waits out Faramis's protection.

reasons: Alpha wants extended fights where he can keep using skill damage, Beta follow-up, and sustain.
Faramis gives Alpha extra safety when Alpha commits with Spear of Alpha or fights multiple enemies.

proof.summary: Alpha wants to stay in the fight long enough to apply sustain and true-damage pressure, while Faramis reduces the risk of Alpha being burst during his commit.
worksBestWhen: Alpha commits while Faramis's Nether Realm is available.
Alpha fights near teammates instead of diving alone.
failureCases: Alpha engages outside Faramis's protection area.
Enemy kites Alpha and waits out the protection window.

reasons: Thamuz wants long brawls, and Faramis helps him survive the most dangerous burst window.
Faramis allows Thamuz to keep pressuring enemies during a teamfight instead of retreating immediately.

proof.summary: Thamuz extends fights through frontline pressure, while Faramis adds protection that helps him continue fighting.
worksBestWhen: Thamuz fights near the team, not isolated far from Faramis.
Faramis casts Nether Realm while Thamuz can still deal damage.
failureCases: Thamuz chases too far from Faramis.
Enemy disengages until Nether Realm expires.
